campbell.title: Campbell City Council — April 21, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to discuss and potentially approve changes to how much local businesses have to pay for their operating licenses. Shannon Dragman from the finance department presented the proposed tax rate changes, and the council held a public discussion before voting on the new ordinance.
keyTopics: Business license tax rates being increased or adjusted
Annual update to Campbell Municipal Code
Public deliberation and council vote on the ordinance
County emergency preparedness involvement mentioned

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a public meeting on March 18, 2026 where residents had a chance to speak about a proposed new rule or law. After hearing from the public, the council introduced the ordinance and skipped the usual first-reading delay, moving it directly to a vote at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held for community input on a new ordinance
Council waived the first reading requirement to speed up the process
Ordinance scheduled for final adoption vote at next regular meeting
Residents had opportunity to comment before council action

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 21, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 21, 2026, to approve meeting records from their previous session and review the city's finances for February. The council authorized the Town Manager to sign several construction and service contracts, including work with QLM, Inc., and approved a resolution to move forward with town improvements.
keyTopics: Approved previous meeting minutes and financial reports
Construction contract authorized with QLM, Inc.
Town improvements resolution adopted
Multiple professional service agreements authorized
Town Manager given authority to execute contracts

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 29, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to organize upcoming agendas for May 5th and May 12th, 2026. Council members reviewed which new items to add to these meetings, set specific times for certain discussions, and checked if any items needed special notice requirements. They also reviewed public records from mid-to-late April.
keyTopics: Finalized the May 5, 2026 meeting agenda and added new items
Set specific time slots for agenda items to keep meetings on schedule
Reviewed public records from April 16-23, 2026
Prepared the draft agenda for the May 12, 2026 meeting

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met on April 30, 2026, to approve meeting minutes and hear from residents during public comment. The main focus was reviewing neighborhood grant requests and deciding how to fund community projects for the 2026-27 fiscal year, along with discussing attendance at neighborhood meetings.
keyTopics: Neighborhood grant funding decisions for 2026-27
Public comment period for residents
Community project funding recommendations
Neighborhood meeting attendance review

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 28, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special evening meeting on April 28, 2026 to interview candidates for various city boards and commissions. Residents were able to watch and comment both in person and online during the session.
keyTopics: Board and commission candidate interviews
In-person public comment opportunity
Online participation option for residents
Special study session format

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 30, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met on April 30, 2026, primarily to approve meeting records from the Economic Development Committee's January and March sessions. The council also opened the floor for residents to speak about any local issues for up to 3 minutes each, though they noted that decisions typically can't be made on topics not officially on the agenda.
keyTopics: Approval of January 29, 2026 Economic Development Committee minutes
Approval of March 27, 2026 Economic Development Committee minutes
Public comment period for residents (3-minute limit)
Economic development matters under committee review

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 21, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council met to discuss allowing large promotional signs to be displayed on buildings around Levi's Stadium during the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The council also approved a new labor agreement with the city's firefighters union covering 2026-2029.
keyTopics: Temporary large signs permitted around Levi's Stadium for World Cup 2026 events
New 3-year contract agreement with Santa Clara firefighters
One agenda item was withdrawn by city staff before discussion

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 21, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a meeting on April 21, 2026 to approve several important financial and operational matters for the city. They discussed the city budget for the next fiscal year, reviewed user fees, and heard a presentation about addressing homelessness in Santa Clara County. The council also approved previous meeting minutes and reviewed upcoming agenda items.
keyTopics: Approved next year's city budget and development fees for FY 2026-27
Updated user fees and service charges for city services
Discussed local homelessness prevention and housing efforts
Reviewed and approved previous council meeting records

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 29, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to discuss several items including leadership elections and a major development proposal. The main focus was a request to split a vacant 1-acre lot on Old Trace Road into 9 residential lots to build 9 new single-family homes and 7 junior accessory dwelling units (smaller secondary homes on the properties).
keyTopics: Subdivision of 1-acre property at 4103 Old Trace Road into 9 residential lots
Proposal to build 9 single-family homes and 7 junior accessory dwelling units
Election of new Council Chair and Vice Chair
Environmental review completed and project exempt from full CEQA analysis
